response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack developer with strong web/backend experience and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments, but the role demands 8 years of C++ and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure/distributed systems. The resume shows only ~6 months of C/C++ work (2014-2015) and primary expertise in TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, not C++ cloud compute platforms. No evidence of cloud compute platform leadership or distributed-systems architecture at the scale required.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with 15+ years across web, mobile, and embedded
Demonstrated mentoring/leadership via workshops, talks, and founding GISCollective
Some low-latency, high-throughput experience at Dunnhumby (millions of req/sec, though in DLang)
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented C/C++ experience vs. 8-year minimum requirement
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed systems architecture experience
Primary stack (TypeScript/Node.js/DLang) misaligned with C++ infrastructure focus of the role

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform/distributed systems scale
